intro: Achillea millefolium, also known as yarrow, is a herbal remedy that has been used for centuries to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and improve digestion. Achillea millefolium glycerite is a liquid extract of the plant, typically made by infusing the flowers and leaves in a glycerin base to create a concentrated and shelf-stable tincture. When taken internally, Achillea millefolium glycerite may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce menstrual cramps, and support overall health and well-being.
side_effects: Achillea millefolium glycerite can have side effects such as dizziness, digestive issues, and allergic reactions. However, more research is needed to fully understand the potential side effects of this herbal remedy. Individuals should consult a healthcare professional before using achillea millefolium glycerite, especially if they have any underlying health conditions or are taking medications.
uses: Achillea millefolium glycerite is used to alleviate menstrual cramps, reduce inflammation, and support digestive health. It is also traditionally used to soothe various skin issues, such as burns and wounds. Additionally, it may help lower fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.

recipe: To make achillea millefolium glycerite for medicinal use, start by harvesting the fresh flowers of the plant, then steep them in a carrier oil such as grapeseed or sunflower oil to create a infused oil. After that, combine the infused oil with a glycerin base, such as vegetable glycerin, in a specific ratio, typically 1:1 or 1:2, and store the mixture in a cool, dark place to allow the glycerin to extract the medicinal properties. Once the mixture has sat for a period of time, usually several weeks, strain it through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and store the achillea millefolium glycerite in a dark glass bottle.
taste: Achillea millefolium glycerite is known to have a bitter and slightly earthy taste. The bitter flavor is often associated with the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, which are compounds found in the plant. The taste can be quite distinctive and is often used in herbal medicine preparations.
